[Theory: The claim "One can crack their teeth biting a Pecan" is likely incorrect based on common sense and general knowledge. Pecans are hard, nut-like seeds that are typically not eaten in a way that would cause teeth to crack. Biting a pecan is a common action, but it is not typically associated with dental damage. The texture and hardness of pecans make it unlikely that they would cause teeth to crack when consumed.]
